name: Bayonne - Marais d'Orx Loop
name_original: Reserve Naturelle du Marais d'Orx - boucle
description: This moderate 30.8-mile road cycling loop near Bayonne offers views of the Marais d'Orx wetlands, the Adour River, and the Pyrenees.
region_intro: Waterfall hiking trails around Bayonne traverse a landscape characterized by dense woodlands, river valleys, and the foothills of the Pyrenees. The region features a mix of rolling hills and natural reserves, providing varied terrain for outdoor exploration. Hikers can expect paths that wind through forests, often leading to impressive cascades. This area offers a natural environment suitable for discovering numerous waterfalls. Best waterfall hiking trails around Bayonne The most popular waterfall hiking route is Cascada El Cubo – circular by the ZEPA Roncesvalles-Sierra Irati, a 4.1 miles (6.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 54 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views of the El Cubo waterfall within a protected natural area. Another top favourite among local hikers is El Cubo Waterfall – Irati Houses loop from Orreaga-Iratiko oihana BBE-KBE, a moderate 7.4 miles (12.0 km) path. This trail winds through the Irati Forest, connecting traditional houses with waterfall views. Local hikers also love the Irati Houses – El Cubo Waterfall loop from Urbeltza, a 3.1 miles (5.0 km) trail leading through forest paths and to the waterfall, often completed in about 1 hour 22 minutes. Hiking around Bayonne is defined by dense woodlands, river valleys, and natural reserves. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult trails, accommodating various ability levels. The routes in Bayonne are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 7,700 reviews. More than 66,000 hikers have used komoot to explore Bayonne's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many waterfall hiking trails are there around Bayonne?
A: There are over 40 distinct waterfall hiking trails around Bayonne, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. These trails traverse dense woodlands, river valleys, and the foothills of the Pyrenees.

Q: Are there easy waterfall hikes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Bayonne offers several easy waterfall hikes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15006834" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Irati Houses – El Cubo Waterfall loop from Urbeltza&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3.1-mile (5.0 km) trail that takes about 1 hour 22 minutes to complete, making it a great option for a relaxed outing.

Q: Are there challenging waterfall hikes for experienced hikers?
A: Absolutely. For those seeking a more demanding experience, there are 10 difficult waterfall hiking trails around Bayonne. These routes often feature greater elevation changes and longer distances, providing a rewarding challenge amidst the region's natural beauty.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the waterfall trails near Bayonne?
A: The waterfall trails around Bayonne are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 7,700 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the serene forest paths, the impressive cascades, and the well-maintained routes that offer a refreshing escape into nature.

Q: Are there circular waterfall hikes available?
A: Yes, many waterfall hikes around Bayonne are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e968307200/cascada-el-cubo-circular-by-the-zepa-roncesvalles-sierra-irati" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada El Cubo – circular by the ZEPA Roncesvalles-Sierra Irati&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 4.1-mile (6.7 km) trail that offers beautiful views of the El Cubo waterfall.

Q: What is the best time of year to visit the waterfalls near Bayonne?
A: The best time to visit the waterfalls near Bayonne is typically in spring (April to June) when snowmelt and seasonal rains ensure the cascades are at their most powerful and the surrounding nature is lush. Autumn (September to October) also offers beautiful foliage and pleasant hiking conditions.

Q: Are the waterfall trails near Bayonne dog-friendly?
A: Many trails in the Bayonne region are dog-friendly, especially those in natural reserves and forests. However, it's always recommended to check specific trail regulations or local signage, and to keep dogs on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers.

Q: What kind of natural attractions can I expect to see along these trails?
A: Beyond the waterfalls themselves, the trails wind through dense woodlands and river valleys. You might also encounter various mountain passes in the broader region, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/852512" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saint-Ignace Pass&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3241506" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Col des Trois Fontaines&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic views of the Pyrenean foothills.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or points of interest near the waterfall hikes?
A: While the focus is on natural beauty, the Bayonne region is rich in history. You might find yourself near historical sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3499819" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Socoa Fort&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6088852" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Citadel of Saint-Jean-Pied-de-Port&lt;/a&gt;, which are part of the broader cultural landscape surrounding the hiking areas.

Q: What should I wear for a waterfall hike in Bayonne?
A: Given the region's varied terrain and potential for changing weather, it's advisable to wear sturdy, waterproof hiking boots, layered clothing, and a waterproof jacket. Even in warmer months, paths near waterfalls can be damp and slippery, so good grip is essential.

Q: Is public transport available to reach waterfall trailheads near Bayonne?
A: Access to some trailheads, especially those deeper in the Pyrenean foothills, may require a car. However, local bus services connect Bayonne to surrounding towns, from which some trails might be accessible. It's best to research specific routes and their proximity to public transport stops before planning your trip.

Q: Are there any hidden gem waterfall hikes that are less crowded?
A: While specific 'hidden gems' are often discovered by local exploration, trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24645042" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Cubo Waterfall – Irati Houses loop from Orreaga-Iratiko oihana BBE-KBE&lt;/a&gt; offer a slightly longer, more immersive experience through the Irati Forest, which can provide a sense of solitude away from more frequented spots.
tips: A very nice road with a separate cycle path. You can really enjoy the river view.
A quiet road with views of the Pyrenees mountains in the distance

faqs.question: How challenging is the Bayonne - Marais d'Orx Loop for road cycling?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as moderate. While it covers a distance of nearly 50 km with around 240 meters of elevation gain, the terrain around Marais d'Orx is generally varied but often features mostly paved surfaces. Some longer or more challenging routes in the area might include unpaved sections, but this specific loop is designed for racebikes.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: The terrain for this road cycling route is primarily paved, suitable for racebikes. You'll experience varied landscapes, from flat sections to more undulating stretches. While the core of the Marais d'Orx reserve is pedestrian-only, the cycling route skirts its edges, offering views of the marshlands from nearby roads.

faqs.question: What are the main sights and viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you can enjoy several notable sights. Keep an eye out for the View of the Adour River, offering scenic river vistas. You might also catch a distant View of the Pyrenees on clear days. The route also passes by the vicinity of the Orx marshes, allowing you to appreciate the unique wetland ecosystem.

faqs.question: Can I cycle inside the Marais d'Orx nature reserve?
faqs.answer: No, cycling is strictly prohibited within the Marais d'Orx nature reserve itself. The reserve is a pedestrian-only area dedicated to observing its rich flora and fauna. However, this road cycling loop is designed to navigate routes around the reserve, allowing you to experience the area's scenery and get close to it. You can park your bike at designated areas near the Maison du Marais and explore the reserve on foot.

faqs.question: What wildlife can I observe while cycling near Marais d'Orx?
faqs.answer: While cycling on roads adjacent to the Marais d'Orx, you can expect views of the marshlands, particularly the Marais Barrage and Marais Burret. This significant wetland area is known for its abundant birdlife and other wildlife. You might observe migratory birds like osprey, purple heron, and greylag geese, as well as turtles and diverse vegetation such as rushes and willows.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is generally during spring and autumn when the weather is milder and the bird migration activity in the Marais d'Orx is often at its peak. Summer can be pleasant, but be prepared for warmer temperatures. Winter cycling is possible, but check local weather conditions as it can be cooler and wetter.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this cycling route?
faqs.answer: Specific parking details for the exact starting point of this loop are not provided, but Bayonne offers various parking options. For general access to the area, you can look for parking in Bayonne or nearby towns that provide convenient access to the regional cycling network. Public transport options are also available in Bayonne.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required for cycling this route?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or fees are required for cycling on the public roads that make up this loop. However, remember that access to the Marais d'Orx nature reserve itself is regulated, and cycling is not permitted inside. There might be an entrance fee if you choose to visit the Maison du Marais or specific visitor centers within the reserve on foot.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route utilizes sections of several official trails. You will find yourself on parts of the SLR Longarisse - Bayonne, Transversale Labenne : Plage - Marais d'Orx, Ondres et sa forêt, Circuit du Marais Burret, and Route de l'Adour. The region also benefits from well-established cycling infrastructure, including sections of the Vélodyssée, a major Atlantic coast cycle route.

faqs.question: Is this a loop cycling route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the "Bayonne - Marais d'Orx Loop" is designed as a circular route, meaning you will start and finish at the same general location, without needing to retrace your path.

faqs.question: Can I bring my dog on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are permitted on public roads and cycling paths, provided they are kept under control. However, if you plan to stop and explore the Marais d'Orx nature reserve on foot, please note that regulations regarding dogs within the reserve may apply. It's best to check the specific rules of the reserve if you intend to enter it with your dog.
